spec_match.rationale: Exercises the spec's logical `block inline` axis tokens, the `grid` token (for 2D wrapping), the `wrap` token, and an RTL flip — exactly the explainer's second example.

interactive.rationale: 8×6 colour picker that recomputes the focusgroup attribute string from four live toggles (logical, rtl, grid-wrap, vertical-only), updates a visible attribute readout, fires a key-event log, and selects a colour on Enter/Space.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a logical-axis grid that flips with `dir` and live keymap toggles; page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Home / End handled in addition to arrows, RTL swap is correct, wrap vs no-wrap is toggleable. Missing: Page Up / Page Down (spec discussion mentions paginated grids), focus on a non-rectangular grid (last row partial), and vertical writing mode (`writing-mode: vertical-rl` should remap block to physical-horizontal — different from `dir=rtl`).

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5637601087193088 and the Open UI explainer.

design_system.rationale: Grid cell colours are computed via `hsl(...)` directly — acceptable because the cells ARE colour swatches, the colours themselves are the content. Outside the swatches, variables drive the chrome. The chip preview uses computed style which is fine.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the writing-mode case
openQuestions.detail: Blurb promises logical axes. RTL is shown but writing-mode (vertical-rl, vertical-lr) isn't, and writing-mode is the more interesting test because it remaps block→horizontal-physical. Add a writing-mode toggle alongside the RTL one.

openQuestions.title: Add Page Up / Page Down keymap
openQuestions.detail: Large grids need page-step navigation — spec discussions mention this. Wire PageUp/PageDown to a configurable row-step (e.g. 4 rows) so the demo covers full keymap, not just per-cell arrows.

openQuestions.title: Show a non-rectangular grid
openQuestions.detail: Real colour pickers have an irregular last row. The current 8×6 is perfectly rectangular. Add a 'remove last 3 cells' toggle so the wrap/end behaviour at an incomplete row is visible.

openQuestions.title: Surface that this is a polyfill
openQuestions.detail: Like the nested-focusgroup-lab, this page contains a hand-rolled keydown handler that simulates focusgroup. Note explicitly that the behaviour shown is the page's polyfill and the native implementation in Chrome 146+ may differ in edge cases.

summary: Solid 2D grid demo with a live attribute readout and RTL swap. Add writing-mode (the real logical-axis story), PageUp/Down, and a non-rectangular row to make it the canonical 2D-focusgroup reference. Note the polyfill explicitly.
